summary: Recent updates to the 2D graphics library, known as Moz2D, have improved memory management by implementing more efficient allocation methods. By using **MakeUnique** and **MakeRefPtr**, the library can now handle pointers more effectively, resulting in better performance and potentially lower memory usage when creating graphic objects. Additionally, adopting modern programming techniques like using _for_each_ for iterations enhances code readability and maintainability. These changes, while not immediately visible to users, contribute to the overall robustness and efficiency of the Firefox browser's graphics rendering capabilities.
